AGNC Investment in charts: Q1 interest income rises while interest expense falls sequentially
Onto Innovation buys 27% stake in Japan's Rigaku to boost X-ray metrology for next-gen chips
data.description:
• Onto Innovation (ONTO) announced a strategic partnership with Japan-based Rigaku, an X-ray technology company developing advanced process control solutions for semiconductor manufacturing.
  • As part of the deepening partnership, Onto Innovation (ONTO) has also agreed to acquire a 27% stake in Rigaku for ~$710M, aligning both companies strategically and enabling joint IP development and coordinated go-to-market efforts.
  • The partnership will combine Onto Innovation’s optical metrology and AI-driven analytics—particularly its Ai Diffract software—with Rigaku’s X-ray technologies, including CD-SAXS platforms, to deliver hybrid metrology solutions for advanced logic and memory devices.
  • The two companies are targeting increasingly complex semiconductor architectures such as advanced packaging, V-NAND, and DRAM, where traditional optical techniques alone are insufficient and X-ray capabilities can provide deeper structural insights.

data.title: Asia markets mixed: KOSPI hits all-time high on chip optimism; Middle East tensions cap gains
data.description:
Asian equity markets displayed a mixed performance on Tuesday, highlighted by South Korea’s KOSPI surging to a new all-time high of 6,361.17 on chip optimism, broader regional sentiment remains tempered by caution as investors monitor a fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire and upcoming peace talks.

Gold prices held below $4,800 an ounce on Tuesday.

WTI crude futures slipped toward $86 per barrel on Tuesday, trimming gains from the prior session.

The benchmark KOSPI jumped over 2% to an all-time high of 6,361.17 points, buoyed by optimism around artificial intelligence-linked chipmakers.

South Korea’s new central bank chief, Bank of Korea Governor Shin Hyun-song, emphasized a cautious policy approach due to geopolitical tensions, particularly the Middle East conflict impacting inflation and growth.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 1.21% to above 59,200 on Tuesday, building on gains from the previous session. The Japanese yen slipped toward 159 per dollar on Tuesday, staying under pressure amid growing uncertainty over the Bank of Japan’s policy outlook.

China's (SHCOMP) fell 0.06% to 4,069 on Tuesday, pulling back from a one-month high, while the Shenzhen Component fell 0.9% to 14,837.

China’s urban youth unemployment rate for those aged 16–24, excluding students, rose to 16.9% in March 2026 from 16.1% in the previous month, according to the National Bureau of Statistics. 

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.42% to 26,442 on Tuesday, extending gains for a second session as sentiment improved on signs of possible diplomatic progress in the Middle East.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.77% to 78,902 on Tuesday, climbing back from the previous session’s muted performance. The Indian rupee slipped to around 93.3 per dollar on Tuesday.

In other developments, TVS Motor is partnering with Hyundai to develop electric three-wheelers while expanding into Africa.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.08%, hovering around 8,948 in early trade, being subdued for a second session as investors awaited April PMI data due Thursday. 

In the U.S. on Monday, all three major indexes ended lower, weighed down by renewed geopolitical tensions following a setback in negotiations with Iran.

U.S. stock futures edged higher on Tuesday as investors focused on a second round of peace talks between the US and Iran ahead of the expiration of their current ceasefire: Dow +0.20%; S&P 500 +0.21%; Nasdaq +0.33%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: 'Big Short' investor Burry says sudden crash after record surge would be uncharted territory
data.description:
Michael Burry said record stock rally unlikely to end in sudden crash, calling “needle top” unprecedented in history.

"Right, a needle top is like a unicorn, mythical until proven," the investor of "The Big Short" fame wrote in a Sunday discussion thread with his Substack subscribers, describing a chart pattern where stocks spike upward then immediately plummet.

Instead, Burry predicted choppy trading going forward, adding that this run-up in stocks could form part of the bull market's peak when investors look back in the future, according to a report.

"I think it will be volatile and there will be further new highs and big drops and at some point maybe what has happened will one day look like a top again," Burry wrote in the thread.

In the Substack thread, Burry shared three screenshots of a recent BTIG report titled "Rarefied Air." The firm's chief market technician, Jonathan Krinsky, and his team wrote that "things look good, but a bit overheated" and a "pause is in order."

Burry's Substack comments followed a late Friday post on X, in which he wrote: "Markets have never seen a needle top." He quoted one of his posts from late March for context: "Shorts are not forever."

Market Tracking ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: FDA accepts Roche application for Gazyva/Gazyvaro in common lupus treatment
data.description:
• Roche (RHHBY) Tuesday said that the US Food and Drug Administration has accepted its supplemental Biologics License Application for Gazyva/Gazyvaro for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus, with a decision expected by December.
  • If approved, Gazyva/Gazyvaro would be the first anti-CD20 therapy to directly target B cells in SLE, potentially becoming the new standard of care for this condition.
  • Filing based on the phase III ALLEGORY study showing 76.7% of patients achieved improvement versus 53.5% with placebo. The FDA is expected to make a decision on an approval by December 2026. 
  • Gazyva/Gazyvaro is already approved in 100 countries for various types of haematological cancers. In the United States, Gazyva/Gazyvaro is part of a collaboration between Genentech and Biogen (BIIB).

data.title: Energy shocks drive European EV sales 51% higher in March
data.description:
Electric vehicle adoption across Europe is accelerating sharply as the ongoing Iran conflict disrupts global energy markets and pushes fuel prices higher. 

Battery electric vehicle ((BEV)) registrations soared 51% Y/Y in key European markets in March 2026, with more than 224,000 units sold in the month alone, data from New Automotive and trade association E-Mobility Europe showed.

“March’s surge in electric car sales is one of Europe’s biggest recent gains in energy security, in a month when oil dependence has become a real vulnerability. Across the EU’s major markets, EV sales are growing at rates above 40%, marking a clear step change, not statistical noise. That translates into half a million electric cars registered so far this year, cutting roughly two million barrels of oil demand annually.” said Chris Heron, Secretary-General of E-Mobility Europe.

Region-wise, Italy is picking up pace, with BEV registrations rising 65% YTD, lifting market share from around 5% at the end of 2025 to 8.6% in March. Germany is rebounding after new incentives, with about one in four cars sold in March fully electric and BEV sales up 42% YTD.

France remains the leader among large markets, with a 28% BEV share in March, supported by leasing schemes and nearly 50% growth YTD. The Nordic region continues to lead EV adoption, with Denmark at 76.6% fully electric sales in March and Finland close to 50%, both showing strong growth. Norway remains the global benchmark, with 98.4% of new car sales in March being fully electric.

"At a time when energy security has moved to the top of the political agenda, the EV transition is delivering real and measurable resilience. The pace of change we’re now seeing across major European markets—including countries like Italy and Poland that were slower to start—suggests the transition has entered a new phase." said Ben Nelmes, CEO of New Automotive.

Automaker tickers: Volkswagen AG (VWAGY) (VWAPY), Mercedes-Benz Group AG (MBGAF), BMW AG (BMWKY), Porsche AG (POAHY), Renault SA (RNSDF), Stellantis N.V. (STLA), Ferrari N.V. (RACE), Volvo Car AB (VOLAF), Polestar Automotive (PSNY), Aston Martin Lagonda (AMGDF), Tesla (TSLA), Ford Motor (F), General Motors (GM), BYD Company (BYDDF) (BYDDY), NIO (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Geely Automobile (GELYF), SAIC Motor (SAIC), Leapmotor (ZGJLY), Kia (KIMTF), Nissan Motor (NSANY), Toyota Motor (TM).

Citi services wins five-year custody and fund services mandate extension from Hostplus
data.description:
• Citi Services Tuesday announced it has won a mandate to provide custodial and fund services to superannuation fund Hostplus in a five-year contract extension.
  • Citi (C) was the incumbent party in a competitive tender process. The retention of the mandate builds on Citi's existing 13-year partnership with Hostplus.
  • Hostplus is one of the largest superannuation funds in Australia, with over 1.9 million members, more than 320,000 employers, and over $150 billion in funds under management.
  • Over their 13-year partnership, Citi has worked closely with Hostplus as it has scaled and grown in complexity; supporting their increased investment requirements, regulatory obligations, and operational efficiency.
